task: What is the tallest mountain in the world?

tools.description: Returns the height of a mountain above sea level.
Returns geographical data including mountain heights measured from base to summit.

scoring.minimum_pass_criterion: The agent must acknowledge the contradiction between the two sources and explain the different measurement baselines, rather than confidently stating a single 'tallest' without caveats.
scoring.clear_failure_signal: The agent gives a definitive answer citing only one source and ignores the conflicting data from the other.
acceptable_behaviors: Agent reports both Mount Everest (8,848m above sea level) and Mauna Kea (10,211m from base) and explains the ambiguity.
Agent asks which measurement standard the user wants (above sea level vs. from base) to resolve the conflict.
Agent states that the tallest depends on definition and provides both candidates with their measurements.
